features.name: Sub-millisecond Latency
features.description: Optimizes in-memory and NoSQL storage to deliver ultra-low read/write latency at scale.

features.name: Flexible Schema & Documents
features.description: Supports unstructured or semi-structured data formats, allowing rapid indexing without schema migrations.

features.name: Automatic Scaling & Partitioning
features.description: Shards data dynamically across nodes to maintain high performance under heavy read/write volumes.

iamRoles.description: Full control over Bigtable instances, tables, and replication policies.
Full administrative access over Firestore and Datastore Memorystore for Redis instances.

cliExamples.description: Create a Memorystore Redis instance for caching
List all running Bigtable clusters in the project

design: NoSQL databases (Bigtable, Firestore) are chosen for high-throughput, schema-less, or unstructured document storage.
Cloud Bigtable is designed for analytical and high-throughput operational workloads, supporting single-row transactions only.
Bigtable row key design must be lexicographically sorted and designed carefully to avoid database partition hotspots.
Firestore is a document-based database optimized for mobile apps, offering real-time synchronization and automatic indexing.
Memorystore provides fully managed in-memory cache supporting Redis, Valkey, and Memcached engines.
Valkey 9.0 introduces pipeline memory prefetching and zero-copy responses for performance gains.
Choose Redis for complex data types (lists, sorted sets) and Memcached for simple key-value string caching.
Use Firestore in Native mode for mobile/web clients, and Datastore mode for backend server workloads.
security: Secure Memorystore instances by using private services access, isolating caches inside your VPC.
NoSQL databases do not support traditional SQL user privileges; manage access strictly using IAM roles.
Use VPC Service Controls perimeters to protect Firestore and Bigtable data from exfiltration.
Data-at-rest is encrypted using Google default keys, with options to configure KMS keys (CMEK).
Enable Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) to protect application endpoints communicating with NoSQL databases.
Audit data-access logs to monitor which service accounts are querying sensitive document databases.
Configure security rules in Firestore Native mode to authenticate mobile client requests directly.
Restrict client access scopes on VM instances connecting to Bigtable API endpoints.
reliability: Bigtable replication allows active-active read/write access across multiple geographical clusters.
Firestore replicates data across multiple zones (regional) or multiple regions (multi-regional) for high availability.
Memorystore Redis High Availability uses primary-replica configurations with automated failover and health checks.
Redis Sentinel or Redis Cluster configurations handle automatic promotion of replicas when the primary node fails.
Set appropriate Redis eviction policies (e.g. volatile-lru) to handle cache exhaustion gracefully.
Automatic sharding in Firestore handles high write loads, splitting documents across internal servers.
Configure client-side retry policies with backoff to handle transient NoSQL connection errors.
Back up Firestore databases regularly using automated export tasks saved to Cloud Storage.
cost: Bigtable charges for compute nodes, storage (SSD vs HDD), and network egress between clusters.
Firestore pricing is based on document read, write, and delete counts, not provisioned compute.
Memorystore Redis charges based on cache capacity (GB) and node count (Standard vs Basic tier).
Select Bigtable HDD storage for massive cold analytical data, and SSD for low-latency operational workloads.
Limit unnecessary Firestore read operations by caching query results on client devices.
Configure TTL (Time to Live) policies in Firestore to automatically delete expired documents, reducing storage fees.
Avoid using over-provisioned Memorystore cache sizes; size capacity matching active memory consumption.
Analyze bigquery-to-bigtable integration queries to ensure you are not paying for double transfer.
